quotes.quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.
quotes.rationale: This identifies a novelty evaluator that uses an LLM connected to scholarly and web search tools, with the operational consequence of discarding similar ideas.

quotes.quote: After the completion of each experiment, Aider is then given the results and told to take notes in the style of an experimental journal. Currently, it only conditions on text but in future versions, this could include data visualizations or any modality. Conditional on the results, it then re-plans and implements the next experiment. This process is repeated up to five times.
quotes.rationale: This channel evaluates experiment results textually, records notes, and routes the outcome into replanning and implementation of the next experiment.

quotes.quote: To evaluate the LLM-based reviewer’s performance, we compared the artificially generated decisions with ground truth data for 500 ICLR 2022 papers extracted from the publicly available OpenReview dataset (Berto, 2024).
quotes.rationale: This reports a benchmark-style evaluation of an LLM reviewer against OpenReview ground-truth decisions.

quotes.quote: Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.
quotes.rationale: This describes an operational limitation of the automated reviewer relative to standard reviewing.

quotes.quote: Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.
quotes.rationale: This describes a modality limitation of the reviewer: it cannot inspect figures directly.

quotes.quote: Review ensembling (5 reviews) and subsequent meta-aggregation, on the other hand, did not affect the reviewer’s performance, but can reduce variance.
quotes.rationale: This reports a reviewer reliability-related finding for review ensembling and aggregation.

quotes.quote: Finally, The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.
quotes.rationale: This identifies the memory-admission path for completed ideas and reviewer feedback into an archive.

diagnostics.consequence: Novelty-based ideation control may reject or admit ideas without a reported channel-specific reliability basis.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.

diagnostics.statement: The packet reports an implemented novelty filter but does not report reliability evidence for that exact novelty-filtering channel.

diagnostics.consequence: Reviewer assessments can miss evidence contained in figures and cannot resolve ambiguities through author interaction, limiting the basis for terminal paper judgments.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.
Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.

diagnostics.statement: The automated reviewer is reported to lack rebuttal questioning and vision-based figure inspection.

diagnostics.consequence: Archived findings may be treated as more settled than the packet supports, especially if downstream users rely on the archive without independent follow-up.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Finally, The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.

More generally, we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value. Instead, we advise treating generated papers as hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on.

diagnostics.statement: The system archives completed ideas and reviewer feedback even though the packet cautions against taking the generated scientific content at face value.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.
Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.

recommendations.proposed_action: Add reviewer inputs or checks that cover figure content and rebuttal-style clarification before relying on automated reviewer judgments.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: All terminal reviewer assessments for papers with figures or unresolved questions include evidence from the added figure and clarification checks.
success_check.evidence_needed: Reviewer logs showing figure inspection evidence and clarification handling for applicable papers.
success_check.observable: Terminal reviewer records include figure-grounded observations and rebuttal-style clarification outcomes.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.

recommendations.proposed_action: Evaluate the novelty filter directly against a labeled prior-art similarity set before using its discard decisions as search control.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: A documented property-specific evaluation reports false discard and false admission rates for the novelty filter.
success_check.evidence_needed: Labeled idea-to-literature similarity cases and recorded filter decisions.
success_check.observable: Novelty filter performance report with error rates on the labeled similarity set.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Finally, The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.

More generally, we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value. Instead, we advise treating generated papers as hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on.

recommendations.proposed_action: Mark archived completed ideas and reviewer feedback as provisional hints requiring practitioner follow-up.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Every archived generated paper or finding carries an explicit provisional status until follow-up evidence is recorded.
success_check.evidence_needed: Archive entries showing status labels and any linked follow-up evidence.
success_check.observable: Archive records distinguish provisional hints from findings with practitioner follow-up.

policy_type: closed computational feedback for novelty filtering
partial computational feedback through experiment-note memory update and candidate revision
open terminal assessment for automated paper review
partial computational feedback through archive memory update
